positive_reviews: This product is a gem for my maturing skin; both body and face. I purchased this product for my crepe arms and neck. However, it also worked beyond my expectations on my face too, as it sort of felt like it tightened my face {not a bad thing at all}. It reduced the puffiness under my eyes, and gave a very subtle 'lift' to my outer mouth, eyes, and forehead, where my wrinkles and sagging skin are the most profound. That said, it took me a while to appreciate this lotion because it does leave a slight tackiness after applying, which I don't care for - but I now love. After a few hours of it setting in - my skin feels soft and hydrated, which has been the biggest reward of this product thus far. I use this lotion about three times a week, not every single day or night. However, I can see it being used as a facial night cream, and then being beneficial in the morning whether one wears makeup or not. Again, I rarely wear makeup, so I can't provide an opinion one way or another about that aspect. For the price, and compared to many other products that I've tried and wasted money on - this stuff is the best and has worked so well for my skin, whether as a full body lotion or face. {I use it as both.} It doesn't leave my skin overly dewy or overly dry, so for ME - it works perfectly, and I struggle with balancing very dry skin in the winter months. It's not immediately obvious like it has been for so long. You have to get out in the bright sunshine to even see it, and it still isn't noticeable at a casual glance. I wish I'd taken before and after pictures, but I just didn't expect it to work this well. Thanks Gold Bond!

I could see a noticeable difference on the arm I used the Age Renew on. It was tighter than the other arm. I am satisfied with the results. It also has made my wrinkles on my face less noticeable. If you use this on your face watch out using it around the more sensitive skin areas. It irritated my skin slightly around my nose and near corners of my mouth. This subsided with continued use. It didn't irritate anywhere else. I also use it on my lower legs and also saw a noticeable difference. No crepe skin there but my pores are smaller and the skin looks smoother. I like the price when compared to the Big name brand lotions and cremes.

I had purchased Gold Bond Ultimate before and been satisfied with it, so I did not read the reviews prior to my current purchase in April 2025. But the bottles I received did not say "Ultimate". Then I looked at the reviews. One said, "The picture i the ad is for the old "Ultimate" formula, what showed up was the new, far inferior formula. This is a failure by Gold Bond by changing the formula and they actually lie and say that it's the same in new packaging, which it's not. It's also a failure by the seller since they are showing the old container and shipping the new product." Another said, "Doesn't feel like it used to. The pictures show the old bottles but you get the new formula. Its greasier and not at all the same." Gold Bond is a Sanofi product, so I left a message on the Sanofi website saying their product was being misrepresented on Amazon. A day or two later, I got a phone call from Sanofi. The courteous gentleman listened to my story, asked for the bar code of the product I received, then assured me that it was exactly the same product but with a different name. When I looked at the ingredients, they did conform to the description for Ultimate. So I believe him that it is in fact the same product. I think dropping 'Ultimate' from the name was a dumb marketing move, particularly since they have several products in the Gold Bond line. But this rating is for the product, not for the marketing. As for customer service, it was first-rate. Five stars.

We always keep Gold Bond Friction Defense Anti-Chafing Sticks on hand ever since we both suffered from a horrific case of chafing while on vacation. We had been walking around in wet swim shorts, which irritated our thighs. We both waddled for quite a while until the chafing cleared up. Gold Bond Friction Defense is the first product we purchased to prevent chafing and have continued to use it over the years because it works well. We are proactive in applying it, instead of waiting for chafing to start. I highly recommend you do the same. It gets packed for vacation, right along with the sunscreen. We throw it in a bag to carry with us if we will be away from our resort. The friction defense is white in color, but goes on completely clear. It has never left marks on our clothing. It creates a layer of product on the skin that helps the skin to glide smoothly. It includes aloe, which is soothing to the skin and helps speed healing if any chafing has already started. The listing shows containers with a gold lid and says it is a new look. I received containers with red lids, which matches what we have always purchased in the past. Three 1.75 oz containers come in the set for the current price of $18.87, making each item cost $6.29. That price is comparable to what we usually pay for this product. I think it is a good value because it is effective at preventing chafing.

negative_reviews: I've stayed silent long enough Lol. I'm searching amazon again for a body powder spray and this is one of a limited selection of products. . Then I'm going through my cabinets today and see I already have it. Great. I wondered why I wasn't using it since I'm searching for a body spray again. I found out pretty quickly. The nozzle is barely making a sound. I shake it. I turn the nozzle all different ways. I press even harder and some powder sputters out. Then doesn't work again. If I run the nozzle under hot water and shake it like my life depended on it...oh and press the nozzle super hard like my life depended on it, I might get a puff of white powder, then a spray mist for 3 seconds and and then nothing until I repeat that process...so this is why I never ended up using the can in my cabinet. I originally bought the spray in Dec 2021 and it said "NEW" on the can. Who knows if that's true because it also said "NO MESS" in all caps as I stand there with white powder puffs on my body. So I look on Amazon today to see if the reviews got better more recently...like maybe fixed the issue. Logical, but no.People are still complaining of the same exact issue 1.5 years later. Shame on the manufacturer. They're a big pharma company and can't get this right? Oh, my bad. It's the consumer division. I hope they can at least get their meds right. I'd love to be a product manager there. "Hey guys. I know how we can sell more of this product even though the formulation causes the nozzle to jam. We could change the packaging and make people think the product improved." Come on. If your product doesn't work, take it off the shelf or reformulate it. Don't redo the labeling to sell more of it like you don't know what's going on. And why why why can't someone make an unscented body deodorant spray? I feel like I'm the only person in the world that wants to do yard work but not walk around outside for hours all sweaty. Some geographic locations have alot of bugs. Go figure. Scent and bugs don't mix. I will say the scent of this spray isn't too overpowering...but it's hard to tell for sure since I only get a few puffs out if the can. I'm normally not this negative...but this is frustrating.

I will live and die by this brand and this friction defense stick! I carry it with me EVERYWHERE I go in the summer. It's small enough to throw in a gym bag or medium sized purse (similar to the size of a stick of deodorant). I apply to my inner thighs when wearing shorts to prevent chaffing. I DO reapply occasionally when taking long (3-5 mile) walks/hikes, or when I am at the beach around abrasive sand and salt that takes it off. Just like you would reapply sunscreen. This has been a lifesaver for me, and so much more affordable than the brands marketed to women with just as good if not better quality. The scent is pleasant and unisex, very mild and almost unnoticeable...I would compare it to a classic dove soap / aloe vera lotion mix.

This body powder is an absolute staple in my daily routine. The fresh clean scent is light and refreshing without being overpowering. I love that it is talc free and includes soothing ingredients like aloe and chamomile. It keeps my skin feeling dry, soft, and comfortable all day long. The powder goes on smoothly and leaves no messy residue, which is a huge plus. It is perfect for me to use after a shower, workout, or anytime I need to freshen up. I really appreciate how gentle it is on my skin while still keeping me feeling cool and confident. The 10 ounce bottle lasts a long time and is a great value for how well it works. Overall, this is a high quality body powder that makes staying fresh feel effortless for me.
negative_reviews: I was having issues with sweating between my legs at night from a medication and I decided to try Goldbond. I wanted a more gentle version and no menthol, so I thought this would be a good option. Even got a large powder puff to apply it so I could avoid powder everywhere. The first 2 nights were fine. The 3rd night I woke up with the full body reaction that happens to me when a skincare or haircare product isn't working. Even if I've only applied it to one area, my whole body will light up. So, at 1AM I was up changing the sheets and taking a shower. Finally had to go to a late-night gas station for Claritin just so I could comfortably sleep. Not sure what ingredient caused the reaction. Maybe it was "just" the lavender, which can be an annoying addition. I'm sure this works for many people, but if you tend to be sensitive to skincare products, even the ones touted as gentle, I would use this with caution. Something I learned is that the product might work the first time, but it's the second or third time it'll get you. I thought what was more-so an issue was developing a sensitivity over months. The more you know :)

Summer in Texas is a whole different kind of suffering, and when I sweat too much, my skin gets very angry. I've tried so many things to solve the problem, but nothing worked until I started using Gold Bond Medicated Powder. The problem is that powder is kinda messy to apply every morning, so I had hoped this spray would be cleaner and easier. I was sorely disappointed. The spray is definitely easier to control than powder, and it doesn't leave white marks on the inside of my clothes. But if my skin is even slightly irritated, so little that it's not even red or visibly angry, this spray stings like hellfire. Worse, it doesn't seem to work as well as powder does. It absorbs some but definitely not all sweat, not like the powder does. And that means that by the end of the day, I'm dealing with red, itchy, burning skin again. Needless to say, I'm back to using the regular old powder, and my skin is grateful.

It's menthol and corn starch (gold cap, not red). I'm not going to get into the debate about how totally overblown the danger of talcum powder was (funny it's still used in a variety of cosmetics). But corn starch presents issues of it's own. Not least of which is that it just doesn't work for some applications. Like at all. Not what I intended to buy.

positive_reviews: I have been using this anti-chafe stick for about eight weeks, keeping one in the bathroom, one in my gym bag, and one in the car. The stick format makes it simple. It goes on like a deodorant, glides without drag, feels like a light moisturizer, and dries clear. There is no scent and no greasy film, and I have not had a single mark on clothes. Performance has been excellent for everyday use. Inner thighs on long walks stay comfortable, waistbands do not rub, and hot spots on feet inside new shoes are a nonissue. It has also been great under a tool belt and along backpack straps. The aloe feels calming on skin that is already a little irritated, and the finish is closer to matte than shiny, so it never feels sticky. For workouts and hot, humid days, a quick reapply keeps the barrier going, especially if I am sweating a lot or if fabric gets soaked. That is the only extra step I have needed. The sticks are a practical size, the caps stay put, and the texture stays smooth all the way down. As a three pack, this is easy to stash where I need it and the value is strong for how long each stick lasts. No mess like powder, no smell, and it actually prevents the rub. This has become an easy yes for daily comfort and travel.

This is THE BEST lotion and lotion brand I have ever used. For reference I have eczema on my hands and between my fingers. It tends to flare up during cold weather, when they're dry, and when I'm stressed. I've tried medicated lotions specifically tailored for eczema but they were either too pricy or made my flare up worse. This lotion calms my flare ups instantly and is extremely moisturizing. Although you may have to use multiple pumps for it to to lather since it's quite thick - it's totally worth. My hands have never felt so soft and and moisturized. I feel confident enough to shake hands with people. If you have sensitive or extremely dry skin or light/mild eczema I highly HIGHLY recommend. This is already my second bottle. Update: If you have sensitive or dry skin I DEF recommend the healing and aloe gold bond in comparison to this one but I still love it though

positive_reviews: Iâve tried lots of things to mitigate my super sweaty feet and this is what Iâm sticking with from now on. The gross details: my feet would sweat so much summer and winter that I couldnât wear sandals because Iâd slip right out of them and hurt myself. Not to mention how gross it was to peel off my socks at the end of the day. I tried wearing socks in sandals (I know I know) so at least I wouldnât slip, changing socks regularly throughout the day, and a few other âfoot deodorantâ type things. Those things worked OK, but this spray has worked the best with the least hassle. Hereâs what I like: â¢ Low scent - I catch a whiff when I apply it, but I donât want a strong flowery smell coming from my feet, that would be a little too try-hard. â¢ Easy applicator - I tried a few other sprays that had some more traditional toggles at the top that would break, fatigue my hand, or just be a plain hassle. This one is wide, secure to the can, and doesnât take too much effort. â¢ Easy application - I hardly have to swivel my ankle or spread my toes to get my feet covered and reach all the crevices that normally get real swampy. Itâs got a powerful spray that can get between my toes easy. â¢ Invisible - as long as you donât focus the spray on one spot you probably wonât notice thereâs anything on your feet at all. (Iâm a pale white-skinned person.) â¢ Non-irritating - a lot of body products make me break out, develop rashes or itches, or otherwise irritate my stupidly-sensitive skin, but this spray has so far done nothing. â¢ Effective! - Obviously thatâs what everyone wants to know: does it work? At least for me the answer is a big YES. My feet stay dry for the full 9-10 hours Iâm up and around. Itâs SUCH a relief. On really sweaty days I re-apply again, but I love wearing sandals like a normal person! Hereâs what I wish: â¢ A cap to protect the top - I have a cat and I worry about her sniffing or licking the nozzle and ingesting some, or that if it brushes my dark clothes itâll leave a mark, or that if it falls it will depress and get everywhere. â¢ That it gets everywhere - this is a DUH that Iâm not sure can be changed, but make sure you apply this in a well ventilated area outside, because itâs got a wide spray and the particles will spread everywhere if you donât have some wind to direct it. â¢ Weird feeling at first - it took awhile to get used to the feeling of my feet being covered in a layer of something dusty. It doesnât bother me much anymore but I do give my feet a quick wash at the end of the day or if Iâm going to settle down on something I like on my feet like a fuzzy blanket. â¢ It can leave marks - I donât think this will stain, but my feet did leave a faint white mark on a black blanket, so be cautious of that. â¢ Travel size! - I love this so much I wish there was a small purse-sized can I could lug around for re-application. Iâll update this comment if thereâs any big developments, but for the past few months itâs been a godsend! Going to dance in my dry sandals in celebration. :)

The Gold Bond Healing Hydrating Lotion, specifically the 14 oz. variant, is truly a phenomenal product in terms of effectiveness and overall quality. From its impressive hydrating properties to the addition of soothing aloe, this lotion addresses multiple skin care needs in one package. One application a day keeps my skin feeling supple and soft all day long. The immediate hydration effect is visible from the get-go, and my skin texture has noticeably improved since I started using this lotion. The aloe inclusion deserves a special mention. The packaging is solid, and I particularly appreciate the pump, which dispenses the lotion easily. The 14 oz. size is pretty generous, ensuring that you get a decent run out of a single purchase. It might seem a bit pricey, but considering the benefits and the long-lasting nature of the product, itâs a worthy investment. All in all, the Gold Bond Healing Hydrating Lotion with Aloe is a superb product. While the fragrance might not appeal to everyone, the undeniable hydrating and healing benefits it offers make it a five-star contender in my book. Itâs become a mainstay in my daily skincare routine, and I believe anyone who values quality skincare would appreciate it. Thank you for taking the time to read my review. I hope it helps you in making an informed choice. Wishing you the best of luck in your skincare journey, and may you find your ideal product. Happy moisturizing!

positive_reviews: I am extremely pleased with Gold Bond Medicated Original Strength Body Powder. This iconic body powder has been a trusted part of my daily routine for years, and it continues to deliver exceptional results. First and foremost, the medicated formula is a game-changer for keeping my skin comfortable and irritation-free. It provides a soothing and cooling sensation that's especially refreshing, especially in hot and humid weather. Whether it's after a workout or simply to stay fresh throughout the day, this body powder keeps me feeling dry and comfortable. The powder's absorbent properties are remarkable. It quickly absorbs excess moisture, preventing sweat-related discomfort and chafing. I appreciate that it helps reduce friction, making it ideal for various parts of the body, including underarms, feet, and other areas prone to perspiration. The iconic Gold Bond scent is both familiar and pleasant, adding a touch of freshness to my routine. It's not overpowering but leaves a subtle, clean fragrance that I find enjoyable. Moreover, the packaging and application are user-friendly. The flip-top cap allows for easy dispensing of the powder, and the size of the container is convenient for home use or on-the-go freshness. In summary, Gold Bond Medicated Original Strength Body Powder is a must-have for anyone looking for effective and reliable moisture and odor control. Its medicated formula, absorbent properties, and iconic scent make it a standout choice for daily use. I have been a loyal user of this body powder for years, and it has never disappointed. I highly recommend it to anyone seeking a dependable and refreshing solution for staying comfortable and dry.

The Gold Bond men's essential talc free body powder was a fantastic smelling product which could be used for men in their private places to refresh and keep them dry all day. It has since been replaced with their spray powder nightfall scent version which is complete and total failure. Avoid it at all costs. The problems are as follows: 1. The spray nozzle is constantly being clogged and very rarely does it spray consistently. 2. You're getting less product in the spray bottle then you've gotten in the powder bottle, so you're getting worse value for your money. 3. It's meant to be sprayed onto your private areas, but the ice cold jet which emanates from the spray bottle is too painful to use in those areas, which makes it something you're going to avoid or not want to use. 4. Lastly, you can't spray the product invertedly (upside down) because the the bottle will eventually just spray out air with no product. this is difficult to use if you're spraying in your private areas because you can't really hold a bottle upright with your thumb and aim it where it has to go. If this were underarm deodorant that would be fine, but it's not. Going forward, If you can still find the powder, buy as many as you can because they're nowhere to be found and you'll miss them when they're gone.

positive_reviews: I'm a male, and I gotta keep the boys from sticking! I've tried many brands of body power, both talc and starch. I've tried cheap and expensive boutique brands. I've tried highly rated and some off-brands. So far I've always come back to this Gold Bond Comfort white bottle. The powder. The Gold Bond Comfort powder is a starch-based powder, so it's safe. It comes in a large-ish white bottle that's about 2ce the size of typical boutique brands costing 3x as much. I find it lasts for a good long time. The scent. The scent is probably the best I've experienced. I wouldn't describe it as a "manly" scent, like say something leather, or woodsy. It is more "floral" scented, but it doesn't come off as "flowery" but simply "clean" smelling. My wife loves the smell on me. It's not purfumey, but just smells like I've washed and have a clean body. The feeling. In the past I've always preferred the smooth feel of talc to starch-based powders. I really wanted to get away from talc though, as I really want to "encourage" proximity to the nethers, not repel it. This starch-based powder is the best non-talc feeling powder I've found to date. It applies very smoothly. There is no grit whatsoever. It's just soft and smooth. There's no medication, so nothing tingles or irritates my skin, which I like since I have very sensitive skin. And because it's Gold Bond, it's fairly inexpensive compared to other male products and comes in a bottle twice the size, so I don't have to use it sparingly. Gold Bond. Yes, I know. I hate the nasty Gold Bond traditional powders... the ones in the gold or green bottles. I mean, they will serve in a pinch, but... just nasty. This Gold Bond Comfort however... hard for me to come to terms with it being a Gold Bond product. But there it is... I love it, and it's a Gold Bond product. Overall, great quality product, and it will keep the boys from sticking. No batwings all day long. I highly recommend!

I'd love to echo some of the praise found in other comments (the Iraq and motorbike reviews are almost the only ones needed for this great product), but my personal experience with Gold Bond's Medicated Foot Powder was a bit different. Yeah, I had the typical sweaty, smelly feet that just about everyone mentions in their review. Yeah, this stuff keeps that moisture and odor away. Fantastically. It also keeps my feet feeling cool. Five stars for that alone! (For the reviewer that said it was too harsh, he might not have the same troubles with his feet that so many others do. The surprise benefit I got from this powder is related to plantar warts. I'd had a few on each foot for several years. This is caused by a viral infection that was picked up somewhere and nearly impossible to shake. I tried everything to rid myself of the plantar warts. I "subscribed & saved" to the Dr. Scholl's Plantar Wart Removal System-24 ct. , the Compound W Freeze Off Plantar 8 applications kit , and tried just about every home remedy I could find from the Internet (Including duct tape, lemon juice, tea tree oil, etc). I cut them off, sanded them off, froze them, and burned them (with acid). Each cure was only temporary. When I started using this powder, I noticed that the plantar warts slowly shrunk and eventually disappeared. What an added bonus from an already stellar product! If I could rate this another 5 stars, I would. I'm a life-long user of this medicated foot powder from now on!

Iâm a woman of a certain age, so I know Gold Bond from their less glamorous powders in my parentsâ medicine cabinets. About a year or so ago, however, I saw some of their moisturizers getting some hype, so I jumped on the bandwagon. The first product I got was great, and now I grab new products like this one when I see them. I got this product specifically to use under heavier, occlusive moisturizers and for days when I want something lighter. In the sticky summer days, sometimes you just donât want so much on, you know? This is a lightweight, hydrating, water-based moisturizer. It goes on smoothly, with a cooling feel and a very gel/light cream texture. The scent is very light, faintly floral, and will layer well with water scents, citrus, gourmand, or florals, if you can even smell it. It soaks in immediately. This is the lotion to use after any body serum products after a shower. Then, if youâre using an oil or butter, put that occlusive layer on top of this to get that summer shine. Putting this moisture layer on before an oil or butter means really hydrating your skin so it looks glowy, not greasy. Donât miss this step! Additionally, you want soft, moisturized lips? Put a little swipe of this on before your balm or gloss. I have extremely dry lips, and this (and the purple Gold Bond for winter) have stopped any dryness immediately. This one is already in my repurchase list!

After the scare about talc an the lawsuits with shower to shower, I decided to throw away my shower to shower and choose a talc free powder. After reading most of the lower rating reviews, it seemed that the complaints were so trivial (the sprinkler cap broke, doesn't open easy...) that I wanted to give Gold Bond a try and I'm glad I did. The cap is NOT difficult to open and close. The powder is very fine and light so if you just turn the bottle over rather than shaking it out, then it doesn't "go all over the place" and you don't "breathe it in" like some of the lower rated reviews say. I like the fact that the powder is fine because, unlike the thicker powders, there is no caking, clumping or powder residue. The fine texture allows the powder to absorb quickly and easily. There is only a very mild, clean scent but only if you actually sniff the cap because this scent is barely noticeable on the body and undetectable after the powder is absorbed so men don't have to worry about having a feminine scent and women don't have to worry about it clashing with their perfumes or favorite bath and body product. I'm a nurse and after working long shifts, I still felt clean and fresh by the end of the day. I love this powder and will definitely purchase it again.

I never used any foot powder in the past, but when I decided to go sockless this summer and read recommendations online for a powder, particularly Gold Bond, I decided to trust popular opinion and purchased this. And I'm glad I did! It has a faint fairly nice smell, keeps my feet dry, and doesn't seem to ruin my shoes. Some white residue is left behind, but nothing unreasonable, in my opinion, and it's much better than the sweaty and wet mess I would have found otherwise. My feet come out of my shoes still smelling fresh and feeling clean, and that is a wonder for me since mine sweat in just about anything. My only complaint is that the powder gets everywhere when you apply it. You have to be very gentle to not let any clouds of it blow out of the bottle when you set it down, and to apply it to your feet as carefully as possible so it doesn't land on your clothes or shoes. Fortunately it comes very easily out of the carpet, but it might be a problem for those of you who have darker carpeting or rugs. So maybe consider applying in a bathroom instead? In any case, the product works as advertised, and that is the main thing, while everything else is details - that I'm not certain any other powder doesn't also suffer from. So buy with confidence.
negative_reviews: I've stayed silent long enough Lol. I'm searching amazon again for a body powder spray and this is one of a limited selection of products. .
